name: WP Invoice Management
summary: Multi-user invoicing plugin for WordPress, similar to invoice-generator.com.
goals: Create a multi-user invoicing plugin for WordPress.
Enable users to create, manage, and download invoices.
Ensure secure access control for invoices.
primary_features: Custom fields for client details, line items, dates, and financials

phases.name: Project Setup & Foundation
phases.description: Ensure a stable development environment and a solid foundation for the plugin.

phases.name: Core Invoice Functionality
phases.description: Create the central data structure for invoices.

tasks.name: Add Custom Meta Fields for Invoice Data

phases.name: User Roles & Permissions
phases.description: Ensure multi-user requirements are met securely.

phases.name: Frontend Interface & PDF Generation
phases.description: Focus on user interaction and output.

phases.name: API & Advanced Features (Optional)
phases.description: Add advanced functionality for extensibility and better user experience.

phases.name: Testing & Deployment
phases.description: Ensure the plugin is robust and ready for use.

tasks.name: Set up Testing Framework and Write Tests

how_to_run_locally.description: To run the project locally using Docker Compose:
steps: 1. Ensure Docker is installed and running.
2. Navigate to the project root directory.
3. Run `docker-compose up -d` to start the WordPress and MySQL containers.
4. Install PHP dependencies by running `docker run --rm --interactive --tty --volume "$(pwd)/wp-invoice-management:/app" composer install`.
6. Log in to the WordPress admin panel and activate the 'WP Invoice Management' plugin.

architecture.overview: WordPress plugin with custom post types, meta fields, and a class-based structure.

CPT (Invoices).description: Custom Post Type for managing invoice data.
CPT (Invoices).implementation: WordPress CPT API, custom meta boxes.
